_comment: Image-content SPDX allow-list (D-26). Distinct from .licenses/allowed.json (which governs npm transitives). Apt-installed packages are invoked as separate processes inside the worker container -- no linking, no derivative work on the worker binary -- so the GPL family is permitted here even though it remains excluded npm-side (CLAUDE.md PM2/AGPL precedent). Reference: RESEARCH.md A1 + CONTEXT D-26.

_rejected.FSL-1.1-MIT: Functional Source License; source-available, NOT OSI
_rejected.BSL-1.1: Business Source License; source-available, NOT OSI

_notes: Adding a new identifier requires explanation in the PR description.
Removing an identifier in active use (referenced by an apt-licenses.json entry) breaks the audit -- intentional.
The `curl` SPDX id is the curl project's custom permissive license.
